prompt: Ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5 ratio). A Daniell two-beaker cell sits connected through a wire to an adjustable external power source with a prominent control dial. Above the circuit, a glowing horizontal voltage scale runs across the banner with a bright marker sitting on '1.1 V'; arrows curve in opposite directions on either side of the mark, suggesting the cell can flow forward (discharge) or be driven backward (charge). Sleek scientific-cinematic style, orange and cyan accents on a near-black background (#0a0a0a). No text other than the faint '1.1 V' on the dial scale.
alt: A Daniell cell connected to an adjustable external battery with a dial; a glowing voltage scale crosses the 1.1 volt mark, hinting the cell can run forward or backward

prompt: Technical illustration on a dark background (#0a0a0a): three side-by-side panels of the same Zn–Cu cell, each connected to an external battery of different voltage. Panel 1 'Eext < 1.1 V': electron arrow Zn → Cu through the wire, label 'galvanic', zinc dissolving and copper depositing. Panel 2 'Eext = 1.1 V': a meter reading 'I = 0', no electron arrows, label 'no reaction'. Panel 3 'Eext > 1.1 V': electron arrow Cu → Zn (reversed), label 'electrolytic', zinc depositing and copper dissolving. Keep zinc on the left and copper on the right in all three. Orange accent labels and leader lines, clean technical illustration style. No bright background.
alt: Three side-by-side Daniell-cell panels with an external battery: galvanic with electrons Zn to Cu; balanced with I=0; electrolytic with electrons Cu to Zn and reactions reversed

prompt: Ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5 ratio). A glowing single-line string of chemistry symbols ('Zn | Zn²⁺ || Cu²⁺ | Cu') floats horizontally across the upper half. Below it, a real two-beaker electrochemical cell (zinc electrode, salt bridge, copper electrode) sits in shadow, with faint light beams connecting each symbol to the matching real part — electrode, solution, salt bridge. The idea is 'a whole apparatus written in one line'. Sleek scientific-cinematic style, orange and cyan accents on a near-black background (#0a0a0a). No extra text beyond the floating notation.
alt: A single glowing line of cell-notation symbols floating above a real two-beaker electrochemical cell, the symbols lined up with the parts they represent

prompt: Ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5 ratio). A vintage analog voltmeter with a glowing needle swung partway across its dial sits centre, wired to a faint two-beaker galvanic cell on the right. A pulse of light travels along the wire from the cell into the meter, suggesting the cell 'pushing' charge. Sleek scientific-cinematic style, amber needle glow and cyan wire light on a near-black background (#0a0a0a). No text.
alt: A glowing analog voltmeter needle swinging across a dial wired to a two-beaker galvanic cell, light pulsing along the wire

prompt: Ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5 ratio). Centre: a glass hydrogen-electrode tube with bubbling gas and a platinum foil, lit softly. To its right, a vertical glowing scale marked '0.00 V' at the level of the hydrogen electrode, with faint up/down arrows suggesting other metals measured relative to this zero. The mood is 'the agreed sea level for chemistry'. Sleek scientific-cinematic style, cyan and amber accents on a near-black background (#0a0a0a). Only the '0.00 V' label as text.
alt: A standard hydrogen electrode glowing as a zero-marked reference gauge, with other electrode potentials measured up and down from it

prompt: Standard hydrogen electrode apparatus diagram on a dark background (#0a0a0a). A beaker of '1.0 M H⁺' solution; a thick-walled glass tube descends into it holding a black platinised-platinum foil at the bottom; a hydrogen-gas inlet labelled 'H₂(g) at 1 bar' enters the top of the tube and bubbles rise around the foil; a platinum contact wire runs out the top labelled 'to circuit'. Label: Pt foil (platinised), H₂(g) 1 bar, 1.0 M H⁺, E° = 0.00 V. Orange accent labels and leader lines, clean technical illustration style. No bright background.
alt: SHE apparatus: glass tube with platinum foil dipping into 1.0 M H+ solution, hydrogen gas inlet at top bubbling over the foil, contact wire

prompt: Technical illustration on a dark background (#0a0a0a), two labelled cells side by side. Left cell: a copper electrode in 1.00 M Cu²⁺ joined by a salt bridge to a hydrogen electrode (Pt foil, H₂ inlet, 1.00 M H⁺); a voltmeter on top reads '0.34 V'; copper marked '+ cathode', SHE marked '− anode'. Right cell: a zinc electrode in 1.00 M Zn²⁺ joined by salt bridge to an identical hydrogen electrode; voltmeter reads '0.76 V'; zinc marked '− anode', SHE marked '+ cathode'. Orange accent labels and leader lines, clean technical illustration style. No bright background.
alt: Two cells side by side, each a metal half-cell connected through a voltmeter and salt bridge to a standard hydrogen electrode; copper cell reads 0.34 V with Cu as cathode, zinc cell reads 0.76 V with Zn as anode

prompt: Ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5 ratio). A tall glowing vertical ladder/scale runs up the centre-right, with element symbols arranged as rungs: F₂ blazing bright at the very top, then a descending series, down to Li glowing at the bottom. An upward arrow on one side labelled faintly 'oxidising strength', a downward arrow on the other 'reducing strength'. The mood is 'a ranking that predicts every reaction'. Sleek scientific-cinematic style, amber-to-cyan gradient along the ladder on a near-black background (#0a0a0a). Minimal text — only F₂ and Li symbols.
alt: A glowing vertical ladder of elements ranked by reduction potential, fluorine blazing at the top and lithium at the bottom

prompt: Ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5 ratio). A weathered iron surface with patches of red-brown rust, partly covered by a thin sheen of water. Beneath the surface, a faint glowing overlay reveals a hidden tiny galvanic cell — a darker 'anode' patch and a brighter 'cathode' patch linked by a soft current arc inside the metal. The mood is 'rust is electrochemistry eating the metal'. Photorealistic-cinematic macro style, moody lighting, cyan current glow against rust-orange, near-black background (#0a0a0a). No text.
alt: A rusting iron surface under a thin film of water, with a glowing hint of a tiny galvanic cell — anode and cathode patches — beneath the rust

prompt: Technical illustration on a dark background (#0a0a0a). Cross-section of a grey iron bar with a domed water droplet sitting on top. On the left, an 'anode' patch labelled with arrows showing Fe → Fe²⁺ + 2e⁻ dissolving into the water; on the right, a 'cathode' patch labelled O₂ + 2H₂O + 4e⁻ → 4OH⁻ with O₂ entering from the air. A curved arrow inside the metal shows electrons travelling anode → cathode. Above the surface between them, red-brown rust (Fe₂O₃·xH₂O) deposits. Label: Anode, Cathode, e⁻, rust, O₂. Orange accent labels and leader lines, clean technical illustration style. No bright background.
alt: Cross-section of an iron surface under a water droplet showing an anode region (Fe to Fe2+) and a cathode region (O2 reduction to OH-), electrons travelling through the metal, and rust depositing on top
